title: abies commutata
intro: Abies commutata, also known as the white fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to North America, belonging to the fir family (Pinaceae). The bark of the white fir tree contains salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Abies commutata include its ability to reduce fever and soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Abies commutata can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by combining the bark or needles with other herbs or using them separately for their medicinal properties.
